what_it_does: Defines an application-facing design layer that translates slide/deck DNA into mobile-first product screens, flows, states, components, and evidence classes.
observations: Names `dna/application.json` as the machine record and ties the pieces to the gallery's Application product-face group.

Maps slide concepts to app concepts through a translation table: page to screen, zone to region, focal to gold action, seam law to navigation, story-tree to information architecture, registers to states, document chrome to product chrome, and grounding band to thumb zone.
Separates evidence into Deck-direct, Derived From Principle, and Platform constants, with derived records framed as falsifiable until product evidence arrives.
Defines screen, flow, state, component, and chrome stacks for the application face.
Positions The Workshop as the compositional client that uses this app language to browse pieces, turn dials, review takes, and compose.
standouts: The file treats navigation transitions as semantic connectors with distinct verbs such as descend, transport, and gather-out.
It makes evidence status explicit, distinguishing direct deck evidence from derived predictions and declared platform constants.
It describes app IA as a story-tree where home, drilling, and deep links share one structure.
It frames application UI as a continuation of the same design DNA rather than a separate generic mobile kit.
conventions: Uses declarative prose and compact lists rather than implementation detail.
Uses a translation table to bind source-domain terms to app-domain terms and governing laws.
Uses emphatic epistemic labels such as Deck-direct and DERIVED FROM PRINCIPLE.
Uses metaphorical system language: face, ground, DNA, registers, seams, rails, dials, and stacks.
Keeps the file design-spec oriented; no code symbols are defined.
concerns: Several operational terms depend on outside records for exact interpretation, including dials, fusion, tier fusion maps, hotspots, and modes.json.
The evidence section states that no product capture has been read yet, so much of the described face is explicitly predictive.
The file mixes descriptive taxonomy, design rationale, and client framing in one document.
notes: Brief this file as the application-face manifesto that maps deck DNA into product UI screens, flows, states, and component behavior while labeling its evidence status.
questions: What exact fields exist in `dna/application.json` for the screen, flow, state, and component stacks?
What criteria decide when incoming product evidence falsifies or updates a derived record?
How are navigation verbs such as descend, transport, and gather-out represented in implementation or motion specs?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it presents the application/product face as the same DNA resolved onto mobile-first product ground.
intention_signals: It seems intended to give designers and builders a shared interpretive bridge from the slide/deck system into an application UI system, while keeping derived claims falsifiable against later product evidence.
novelty: The file is distinct in treating app navigation, state handling, and IA as translations of deck laws rather than as independent UI conventions.
contribution: Contributes a neutral application-layer vocabulary and mapping: screen archetypes, flow counts, state registers, component inventory, evidence classes, and semantic transition verbs that a fused system would preserve as product-facing interpretation.
summary_for_embedding: This file defines the Application Face, a mobile-first product UI interpretation of deck DNA with mappings for screens, regions, flows, navigation, IA, states, chrome, components, and evidence classes.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to explain how the same design laws resolve into an app interface for The Workshop while distinguishing direct evidence, derived predictions, and platform constants.
